scenario: Quanta Insurance, an insurer that issues 6000 policies per quarter, runs a Camunda 8.8 claim intake form in Web Modeler. The forms team edits the layout multiple times per day, and the team lead wants the latest revision available to underwriters within 1 minute of any change. A new joiner asks the team lead how the developer is supposed to persist edits to the form.
question: What does a developer need to do to save changes made to a form in Web Modeler?

options.text: The developer presses Ctrl+S after each change and waits for the save confirmation toast before continuing to edit the form in Web Modeler
The developer does not need to take any extra action because Web Modeler autosaves every change made to the form as it is edited
The developer commits the form to a Git branch from inside Web Modeler so the latest revision is preserved alongside the BPMN diagram
The developer exports the form as JSON and uploads it back into the project so the cloud storage retains the latest revision of the file

a.text: Incorrect. There is no explicit Save action documented for forms in Web Modeler. The docs state that the developer does not need to do anything because every change is autosaved by Web Modeler as it is edited.

b.text: Correct. The quickstart states that to save a form in Camunda 8, the developer does not have to do anything because Web Modeler autosaves every change made. The latest revision is always the one rendered to underwriters at runtime.

c.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler does not require a manual Git commit to persist a form. The autosave behaviour persists every change made to the form as it is edited.

d.text: Incorrect. Exporting and re-uploading the form is not part of the documented save flow. Web Modeler autosaves every change, so the cloud project always reflects the latest revision.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 quickstart explicitly states that to save a form in Web Modeler the developer does not have to take any action. Web Modeler autosaves every change made to the form, so the latest revision is always available without an explicit save step or external persistence mechanism.
